zone_section_start.title_label_raw: R-4B ZONE: DUNBRACK MULTI UNIT ZONE

zone_class_reference.summary: R-4B is an established class of use zone.

permitted_uses.use_name: uses permitted in the C-2C Zone
any use accessory to any of the foregoing uses

prohibitions.summary: No development for any purpose other than one or more uses set out in Section 34B1.

incorporated_use_requirements.summary: Townhouses shall comply with the requirements of the R-2T Zone.

height_and_form_controls.value_text: As shown on Map ZM-34, Maximum Height in the Dunbrack Multi Unit Zone

controls.condition_text: elevator enclosures and mechanical penthouse are set back from the roof edge a minimum of 5 metres
elevator enclosures and mechanical penthouse do not exceed 5.5 metres above the maximum building height
elevator enclosures and mechanical penthouse are limited to a maximum area of 30 percent of the total roof area on which they are located
tower portion shall not exceed a building depth or building width of 35.0 metres
tower portion shall not exceed a floor area of 750 square metres per floor

height_and_form_controls.condition_text: for buildings with a streetwall width no greater than 8 metres
for buildings with a streetwall width greater than 8 metres, measured in streetwall portions no wider than 8 metres

controls.condition_text: any mid-rise portion of a building shall have a streetwall stepback above its maximum streetwall height of at least 3 metres
any tower portion of a building shall have a streetwall stepback above its maximum streetwall height of at least 4.5 metres

height_and_form_controls.condition_text: subject to Section 34B15, a building shall have a stepback above its maximum streetwall height of at least 3 metres for building walls that face a property zoned R-1 or R-2 Zones
no streetwall stepback is required for up to 20 percent of the building width along either a front or flanking lot line

controls.condition_text: surface parking lots or above ground parking structures shall only be placed in rear yards or side yards
controls.exception_text: subject to Section 34B38, parking in a side yard shall be at least 9 metres from the public street and shall have a maximum width of 12 metres

controls.condition_text: walkways shall connect all building entrances to a public sidewalk or to a public street
where buildings abut a public street, the main building entrance shall face the street and be visible from the street
subject to 34B17(e), apartment houses on lots abutting any R-1 or R-2 Zones shall have a minimum rear or side setback of at least 10 metres
setback of at least 18 metres from properties zoned R-2T
setback of at least 30 metres from properties zoned R-2T for any portion of a main building within 100 metres of Willett Street

site_layout_and_setback_controls.condition_text: subject to Section 34B17, below the height of the streetwall, no streetwall may be more than 64 metres wide along any streetline for portions of a main building at least 0.6 metres above average finished grade
below the height of the streetwall, the streetwall may exceed 64 metres up to 88 metres along one streetline where the following apply

controls.condition_text: all setbacks, separation distances, and stepbacks are met
no more than two towers are placed on the same podium
above the streetwall, no dimension of a tower exceeds a width of 21.5 metres facing one streetline
a section of the streetwall at least 16 metres in length and extending the full height of the streetwall shall be recessed at least 6 metres

landscaping_signs_balconies_and_design.use_name: apartment house or shared housing uses

controls.condition_text: where a rear or side yard abuts any R-1 or R-2 Zone, the required yard shall be landscaped and a 1.9-metre-high wooden or stone fence or opaque hedge shall be provided along the abutting property line
the landscaped area shall be grassed or may use water features or natural ground covers
controls.exception_text: trees, planters, and shrubs shall be planted at a rate of one tree and three shrubs per 4.5 metres of property line next to the yard

controls.condition_text: any existing tree in a required landscaped buffer may be counted as 2 trees toward clause 34B20(b)

landscaping_signs_balconies_and_design.condition_text: landscaping is required for alteration of an existing building where the alteration increases the ground floor area by at least 20 percent
at least 25 percent of all dwelling units in a new apartment house use, rounded up to the nearest whole number, shall contain at least two bedrooms
notwithstanding 34B22(1), bedroom-count requirements do not apply for residential multi-unit dwelling use beginning construction before 2028-04-01

controls.condition_text: illuminated signs shall not cause a glare or hazard
fascia signs shall not extend beyond the extremities of a wall on which they are affixed
maximum combined size of fascia signs shall be no greater than 10 percent of the total area of the wall to which they are affixed
aggregate area of all window signs shall not exceed 25 percent of the window or glass area of a door
signs on awnings shall not cover more than 25 percent of the awning area and text length shall not exceed 80 percent of the front valance length
no signs shall be permitted on the roof of a building

landscaping_signs_balconies_and_design.use_name: balconies for apartment house and shared housing uses

controls.condition_text: no balcony shall be built closer than 2 metres to a side or rear property line, excluding patios for dwelling units located at grade
balconies, unenclosed porches, verandas, canopies, and awnings may project up to 1.5 metres from any exterior wall at the ground floor
the same elements may project up to 2.0 metres from any exterior wall at the second storey or above, provided total balcony width on one storey is not more than 40 percent of the building wall width
vinyl is prohibited
plywood is prohibited
concrete block is prohibited
exterior insulation and finish systems where stucco is applied to rigid insulation are prohibited
darkly tinted or mirrored glass on the ground floor is prohibited, excluding spandrel panels

landscaping_signs_balconies_and_design.condition_text: grade-related units, work-live units, or a combination of the two are required on the ground floor of building walls that face a public street when the wall is within 9 metres of the public street and residential uses make up more than 50 percent of the width of the ground floor wall

controls.condition_text: at least 70 percent of the ground floor of a building facing a streetline shall commence no lower than 1.5 metres below the streetline grade
at least 70 percent of the ground floor of a building facing a streetline shall not commence any higher than 1.5 metres above the streetline grade

landscaping_signs_balconies_and_design.condition_text: for grade-related unit uses in the streetwall, between 25 percent and 80 percent of each unit's ground floor facade shall consist of clear glass glazing
for at-grade commercial uses in the streetwall, between 50 percent and 80 percent of the ground floor facade dedicated to commercial uses shall consist of clear glass glazing

controls.condition_text: only the listed commercial uses are permitted

subcontrols.use_name: retail of products produced on the premises or associated with a service provided on the premises

controls.condition_text: commercial use may occupy a maximum of 50 percent of the total floor area up to a maximum of 140 square metres
the commercial use shall be located on a ground floor

landscaping_signs_balconies_and_design.condition_text: the principal operator of a business within a work-live unit shall reside in the unit and may have up to three non-resident employees
apartment house uses shall provide 5 square metres per unit and shared housing uses shall provide 5 square metres per bedroom

controls.condition_text: interior amenity space shall include a fitness room or community room of a minimum size of 40 square metres
outdoor amenity space shall include a rooftop patio or deck of a minimum size of 40 square metres or an at grade private open space of a minimum size of 40 square metres

landscaping_signs_balconies_and_design.condition_text: at-grade private open spaces with a contiguous area of 15 square metres or greater shall provide barrier-free access and permanent seating
at-grade private open spaces with a contiguous area of 15 square metres or greater shall provide one or more listed materials for groundcover

controls.use_name: brick pavers, stone pavers, or concrete pavers

landscaping_signs_balconies_and_design.condition_text: at-grade private open spaces abutting public sidewalks shall provide at least one contiguous pedestrian connection of not less than 2.0 metres wide to the public sidewalk

controls.condition_text: notwithstanding subsection 9(d), no minimum parking requirements apply to retail, service, office and restaurant uses
where parking requirements result in a fraction, the requirement shall be rounded down to the nearest whole number

landscaping_signs_balconies_and_design.condition_text: parking lots or parking structures in a side yard shall be screened from view by a 1 metre to 1.5 metre high wooden or stone fence, or row of shrubs parallel to the street
landscaping_signs_balconies_and_design.exception_text: gaps in the fence or row of shrubs are permitted for driveways or walkways

landscaping_signs_balconies_and_design.condition_text: all outdoor lighting shall be oriented away from adjacent properties and public rights of way
all outdoor lighting, other than ground level lighting for pathways, shall be equipped with full cut-off light fixtures
